SEC EDGAR XBRL Financial Statements — Excel Add-in
Version 1.0.0
====================================================

WHAT THIS DOES
--------------
Enter a stock ticker (like AAPL, MSFT, TSLA) and this add-in fetches
real financial statement data directly from the SEC EDGAR database and
fills in three worksheets:

  • Income Statement  (annual + quarterly)
  • Balance Sheet     (annual + quarterly)
  • Cash Flow         (annual + quarterly)

No subscriptions. No sign-up. No internet account needed.
Data comes straight from SEC EDGAR — the official US government filing database.

REQUIREMENTS
------------
  • Windows PC (Windows 10 or 11)
  • Microsoft Excel 2016 or newer (Microsoft 365 also works)
  • Internet connection (to fetch SEC data)

INSTALL — 4 STEPS
-----------------

STEP 1: Save the add-in file
  Copy "SEC_XBRL_Addin.xlam" to a folder on your computer.
  Suggested: C:\Users\YourName\Documents\ExcelAddins\

STEP 2: Install it in Excel
  1. Open Excel (any workbook, or a blank one)
  2. Click the FILE tab (top-left)
  3. Click Options (near the bottom of the left menu)
  4. In the Excel Options window, click Add-ins (left sidebar)
  5. At the bottom, next to "Manage:", make sure it says "Excel Add-ins"
  6. Click the Go... button
  7. In the Add-Ins window, click Browse...
  8. Navigate to the folder where you saved SEC_XBRL_Addin.xlam
  9. Select SEC_XBRL_Addin.xlam and click OK
  10. You should now see "SEC_XBRL_Addin" in the list with a checkmark
  11. Click OK

STEP 3: Enable macros (if prompted)
  When you first open the add-in, Excel may show a security bar saying
  "Macros have been disabled." Click "Enable Content" or "Enable Macros."
  This is required for the add-in to work.

  If Excel blocks it entirely:
  1. Go to File > Options > Trust Center > Trust Center Settings
  2. Click Macro Settings
  3. Select "Disable all macros with notification"
  4. Click OK, then re-open the add-in

STEP 4: Use it!
  Once installed, you have two ways to pull data:

  OPTION A — Keyboard shortcut (fastest):
    Press Ctrl + Shift + S
    Type a ticker (e.g. AAPL) and press Enter

  OPTION B — Macro:
    Press Alt + F8
    Select "PullSECFinancials"
    Click Run
    Type a ticker and press Enter

  OPTION C — Add-ins ribbon tab:
    Click the "Add-ins" tab in the Excel ribbon
    Click "Pull SEC Financials"
    Type a ticker and press Enter

  Note: The ribbon button (Option C) requires an extra setup step using
  the Office RibbonX Editor. Options A and B work without it.

UNINSTALL
---------
  1. Go to File > Options > Add-ins
  2. Click Go... next to "Excel Add-ins"
  3. Uncheck "SEC_XBRL_Addin" and click OK

TROUBLESHOOTING
---------------
  "Ticker not found"
    → Check spelling. Use the official ticker (e.g. BRK-B not BRKB).
    → The add-in uses the same tickers as SEC EDGAR.

  "Cannot connect to SEC servers"
    → Check your internet connection.
    → SEC EDGAR may be temporarily down. Try again in a few minutes.

  "No US-GAAP XBRL data found"
    → This company does not file US-GAAP XBRL with the SEC.
    → Usually means non-US companies or very small filers.

  Nothing happens when I press Ctrl+Shift+S
    → The add-in may not be installed. Repeat Step 2 above.
    → Make sure macros are enabled (Step 3 above).

DATA NOTES
----------
  • Data comes directly from SEC EDGAR with no modifications.
  • Annual data = 10-K filings. Quarterly = 10-Q filings (Q1, Q2, Q3 only).
  • Q4 does not appear in the quarterly section (it is not filed separately).
  • XBRL concept names are shown as filed (e.g. "RevenueFromContractWithCustomer...").
  • Large companies (Apple, JPMorgan) may take 15–30 seconds — this is normal.

  License: MIT

    https://github.com/VBA-tools/VBA-JSON
